summary: Validated P0 data-integrity fix: Unknown/split-child auto-clear guardrail + posted_to_bc_7d KPI + parent-inheritance helper. 8/8 new unit tests pass, 4/4 test_auto_close_confidence_fix, 28/28 test_po_resolution_api, and a new 10-case HTTP regression suite all green. Found + fixed a critical missing-import bug in routers/auto_clear.py that was returning 500 on /api/auto-clear/evaluate/{doc_id} (and /config, /config/threshold) — the hard guardrail was correct in the service layer but unreachable via HTTP.

critical.issue: routers/auto_clear.py was missing imports for evaluate_auto_clear, get_auto_clear_config, update_threshold, get_auto_clear_summary, AutoClearDecision — every call to the module raised NameError → 500 Internal Server Error. FIXED by adding the import block from services.auto_clear_service. After fix, all 3 endpoints return 200 and the unclassified_guard fires correctly end-to-end.

minor.issue: Path specified in the review_request does not exist; actual paths are /api/learning/pattern-health/unified (learning_core router) and /api/intake/learning/pattern-health (intake_learning router). Both return 200. No code change needed, just a docs/path mismatch.

Actual prefix is /api/auto-clear-reprocess (separate router); endpoints are /dry-run, /run, /force-clear-remaining, /clear-junk/dry-run, /clear-junk/run. Confirmed dry-run responds 200 (code path uses same evaluate_auto_clear → guard effective here too).

action_items: Main agent: review the routers/auto_clear.py import fix (committed by testing agent) — this was a latent bug in the checkout, not part of the P0 fix scope.

Consider updating PRD/context note: /api/auto-clear/reprocess is actually /api/auto-clear-reprocess/* and /api/learning/pattern-health is /api/learning/pattern-health/unified (or /api/intake/learning/pattern-health). Review request paths were slightly off.

4 failures in test_validation_auto_clear_readiness_fixes.py confirmed pre-existing (PO-resolution check added earlier in history) — not regressions of this iteration. Recommend main agent either update those fixtures to include resolved PO OR mark them as xfail with explanation.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/auto_clear.py had NO imports for the service-layer helpers it called (evaluate_auto_clear, get_auto_clear_config, update_threshold, get_auto_clear_summary, AutoClearDecision, AUTO_CLEAR_CONFIG) — every endpoint in that router was broken. Added the imports. Root cause was likely a refactor/rename that dropped the import block.
auto_clear_service.py → evaluate_auto_clear guard block (lines 212-270) is tight and well-commented; handles None/empty/Unknown/Other/DEFAULT doc_types and percentage-string confidences. ✓

batch_po_splitter.py → _inherit_parent_and_reevaluate correctly preserves AI-original values as doc_type_from_split_ai / classification_confidence_from_split_ai for audit, only inherits real parent values, and never overwrites existing child extractions. ✓
routers/dashboard.py KPI logic (via /inbox-stats) verified: posted_to_bc_7d and ready_for_post both present as ints (0 and 1 respectively on current data). ✓
Minor: the new guardrail uses meaningful_early < 2; consider documenting this threshold in a constant so future tuning is explicit.
updated_files: /app/backend/routers/auto_clear.py (added missing imports block — criticial bug fix)

success_rate.backend: 100% (50/50 target tests; 4 pre-existing unrelated failures confirmed not regressions)

test_credentials: admin / admin per /app/memory/test_credentials.md (not needed for these backend tests — endpoints are unauthenticated in preview env)
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral TEST_unknown_* and TEST_ap_* docs created in hub_documents and deleted in fixture teardown. No customer data touched. No leftover rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 224 focus: auto-clear unknown guardrail + parent inheritance + posted_to_bc_7d KPI. All passing. Critical finding: routers/auto_clear.py had broken imports — fixed. New HTTP suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter224_unknown_guard_http.py can be reused as regression. Pre-existing failures in test_validation_auto_clear_readiness_fixes.py (4) are NOT regressions — they rely on PO-resolution shortcut that was tightened earlier in history.
rca of the issue: P0 bug: Auto-split children (e.g., Ball Metal *_p11.pdf) classified as doc_type=Unknown + conf=0.0 + 0 fields passed evaluate_auto_clear because the Unknown threshold config had confidence_threshold=0.0 with no require_minimum_extraction. Trivially 0.0 ≥ 0.0 → CLEARED → exported/completed, skipping human review. Fix: (1) services/auto_clear_service.py adds early guard rejecting Unknown/Other/empty doc_types when conf<0.70 OR meaningful_fields<2, returning NEEDS_REVIEW with unclassified_guard_triggered=True; (2) services/batch_po_splitter._inherit_parent_and_reevaluate inherits parent doc_type + vendor_canonical into split children that returned Unknown, forces status=NeedsReview, preserves AI original under *_from_split_ai; (3) routers/dashboard.py widens posted_to_bc_7d query across bc_purchase_invoice_no/bc_record_no/bc_document_no/bc_record_id and multiple timestamp fields (posted_to_bc_at/bc_posted_at/posted_at/updated_utc). Secondary bug discovered during testing: routers/auto_clear.py was missing the import block for the service helpers — fixed here, else the HTTP guardrail path returned 500.
